note:
与匹克设计公司的捕获、锚定和安装板系统类似，Mobile by Peak Design提供了一种将智能手机与各种配件连接的方式，包括三脚架、车架、充电座、自行车车把等。该系统是围绕匹克所谓的“SlimLink”连接器进入的，这是一个巧妙的磁力和物理安装接收器组合，你可以用专用的外壳或通用的粘性背部配件连接到你的手机上。然后，SlimLink可以与软锁和硬锁配件一起使用，这些配件要么单独使用磁铁(软)，要么使用磁铁与物理集水器(硬)相结合，以实现与一系列支架的不同程度的稳定连接。
Peak Design正在Kickstarter上发起众筹活动，但该产品的设计和生产已经达到了高质量水平。它发出了Mobile阵容中一系列产品的媒体样本，包括SlimLink通用手机安装架、车把安装架、折叠三脚架、两个磁性/棒背通用安装垫和车载仪表盘安装。
在过去的几周里，我一直在使用它们，我发现它们的用途和便利性令人难以置信。匹克还提供了iPhone11Pro保护套，但由于我使用的是iPhone11Pro Max，我只是使用附带的尺寸和对齐指南直接将3M靠背的通用板固定在手机上。这个附件非常安全，根本不会给你的手机增加太多厚度(它基本上只提供了足够的间隙，以至于iPhone11Pro的摄像头凸块几乎不会清理桌面)。
它与软锁支架之间的磁性连接足够强，我从不担心它们会松动-我经常使用冰箱上的通用磁性支架，手机一动不动。自行车支架，加上额外的物理插头，在实际骑自行车时是坚如磐石的，支架上的手臂使手机成为骑自行车时的导航设备，无论是纵向还是横向，都是一个很好的位置。
匹克在这个系统的设计上确实超越了自己，但当涉及到三脚架时，这可能是最正确的。巧妙的三条腿的折叠设计很小-整体占地面积比信用卡小，尽管略厚一些-能够将它放在口袋里随身携带，并有一个稳定的平台来拍摄延时照片，这真是令人惊叹。您也可以使用随附的Allen键来调整其稳定性。
车载底座有一个粘性靠背，可以粘在仪表板上，可以放入手机底座/外壳上的SlimLink凹槽中，而不会被物理卡住。它在测试中是稳定和安全的，最好的是，匹克制造了一个可调节的球，让你可以调整手机的方向，使你可以移动它，但它不需要任何额外的拧紧。到目前为止，我对通用支架唯一的抱怨是它与我的Nomad基站专业充电器不兼容，尽管匹克表示正在测试无线充电器的配件，并将就未来的兼容性提出建议。同时，Peak Everyday手机壳与许多齐充电器兼容。
匹克表示，这些设计可能会发生变化，当然，MagSafe对公司来说是一个惊喜，就像它对世界其他地方一样。匹克仍计划为该系列生产iPhone12保护套，并表示其所有软锁配件也将与Apple MagSafe手机和MagSafe保护套一起使用。苹果的MagSafe配件，像钱包一样，也将同样附在MagSafe手机上。
这可能是苹果在竞争产品上市前就宣布淘汰的时刻之一，但匹克的移动系统设计实际上让它们变得相形见绌-并提供了与原本可以利用MagSafe提供的优势的手机和设备非常相似的好处。在这种情况下，苹果可能会宣布一些东西，让竞争对手的产品在上市前就过时了，但匹克的移动系统设计实际上让它们变得相形见绌。
Kickstarter活动今天启动，匹克相信它将能够从2021年春季开始发货移动系统外壳和配件。
note_en:
Similar to Peak Design’s Capture, Anchor and mounting plate system, Mobile by Peak Design offers a way to connect smartphones to all kinds of accessories, including tripods, car mounts, charging stands, bike handlebars and much more. The system is entered around what Peak calls its “SlimLink” connector, which is a clever combo magnetic and physical mounting receiver that you can attach to your phone either with dedicated cases, or a universal sticky-backed accessory. SlimLink then works with both soft-lock and hard-lock accessories, which use either magnets alone (soft) or magnets combined with physical catchments (hard) for varying degrees of stable connection with a line of mounts.
 Peak Design is launching on Kickstarter with a crowdfunding campaign, but the product is already designed and produced to a high level of quality. It sent out media samples of a range of products in the Mobile lineup, including a SlimLink universal phone mount, a handlebar mount, the folding tripod, two magnetic/stick-backed universal mounting pads, and an in-car dashboard mount.
  I’ve been using these for the past couple of weeks and have found them to be incredibly versatile and convenient. Peak also supplied an iPhone 11 Pro case, but since I’m using an iPhone 11 Pro Max, I just affixed the 3M-backed universal plate directly to my phone using the included sizing and alignment guide. The attachment is incredibly secure, and doesn’t add very much thickness to your phone at all (it basically provides just enough clearance that the iPhone 11 Pro’s camera bump barely clears table surfaces).
 The magnetic connection between it and the ‘soft-lock’ mounts is strong enough that I’m never worried about them coming loose – I’ve used the general purpose magnetic mounts on my fridge often, and the phone hasn’t moved. The bike mount, with its additional physical prongs, is rock solid while actually biking around, and the arm on the mount puts the phone is a great position for acting as a navigation device while biking around, in both portrait and landscape orientations.
 Peak has really outdone itself with the design of this system, but that is maybe most true when it comes to the tripod. The clever, three-legged folding design is tiny – smaller overall footprint than a credit card, though a bit thicker – and it’s amazing to be able to carry this everywhere in a pocket and have a stable platform for taking time-lapse photos. You can adjust its stability using the included Allen key, too.
 The car mount has an adhesive backing for sticking to your dashboard, and fits in the recessed SlimLink slot on the phone mount/case without physically catching. It’s stable and secure in testing, and best of all, Peak has made the adjustable ball that lets you orient your phone just the right amount of stiff that you can move it but it doesn’t require any additional tightening. My one complaint thus far with the universal mount has been that it isn’t compatible with my Nomad Base Station Pro charger, though Peak says it’s testing the accessory with wireless chargers and will advise as to compatibility in future. The Peak Everyday phone case, meanwhile, is compatible with many Qi chargers.
  Peak says these designs are subject to change, and of course, MagSafe was a surprise to the company just as it was to the rest of the world. Peak still plans to create iPhone 12 cases for the range, and says that all of its soft-locking accessories will also work with both  Apple   MagSafe phones, as well as MagSafe cases. Apple MagSafe accessories, like the wallet, will also likewise attach to MagSafe phones.
 This could’ve been one of those moments where Apple announces something that renders a competing product obsolete before it even gets to market, but Peak’s Mobile system design actually makes them complimentary – and provides very similar benefits to phones and devices that otherwise would’ve have been able to take advantage of what MagSafe offers.
 The  Kickstarter campaign launches today, and Peak believes it will be able to ship the Mobile system cases and accessories starting in Spring 2021.
title: Mobile by Peak Design是一款全新的、完整的移动安装解决方案，可为日常生活带来便利
title_en: Mobile by Peak Design is a new, complete mobile mounting solution for everyday convenience

这家成立于2016年的计算机视觉初创公司于2017年推出了一项名为Pholio的人工智能照片分类器服务-将这项服务定位为智能手机用户在数字记忆上收回代理机构的一种方式，而不必将他们的数据交给谷歌等云巨头。
自那以后，它将Pholio定位为大型图像和视频收藏以及实况流(如美术馆或广播公司拥有的那些)的“专业搜索和发现平台”，并推出了第二个工具，该工具由其深度学习平台提供支持。该产品名为Secure Redact，提供以隐私为重点的内容审核工具-使其用户能够在视觉内容中查找和编辑个人数据。
它给出的一个示例用例是，执法部门将人体摄像头录像匿名化，以便将其重新用于培训视频或准备作为证据提交。
首席执行官西蒙·兰德尔告诉TechCrunch：“几年来，Pimloc一直致力于处理多样化的图像和视频内容，为面临分类、审核和数据保护挑战的企业提供支持(图像库、美术馆、广播公司和CCTV提供商)。”
通过我们在视觉隐私方面的工作，我们发现了服务市场上的一个重要缺口，这些服务使企业和政府能够在安全镜头上管理大规模的视觉数据保护。Pimloc已经在这一领域工作了几年，建立了能力和产品，因此Pimloc现在只围绕这一使命开展业务。“。
Secure Redact有两个组件：第一个(自动)步骤，检测视频内容中的个人数据(如面部、头部、身体)。最重要的是兰德尔所说的一层“智能工具”--让用户快速查看和编辑结果。
“在接受和编辑之前，所有的检测和轨迹都是用户可以审核和编辑的，”他解释说，并补充道：“个人数据不仅延伸到面部，还延伸到其他物体和场景内容，包括身份证、纹身、手机屏幕(穿戴在身上的相机习惯于在佩戴者打字时在手机屏幕上接收信息，或者在笔记本电脑或笔记本上记录敏感笔记)。”
他提到的编校工具的一个具体用户是布里斯托尔大学。在那里，由计算机视觉副教授Dima Damen博士领导的一个研究小组正在参与一个由12所大学组成的国际联盟，该联盟的目标是收集关于自我中心视觉的最大数据集-在将视频数据集提供给学术/开源使用之前，需要能够将其匿名。
在法律方面，兰德尔说，Pimloc提供了一系列数据处理模型，从而迎合了数据处理方式/地点的不同。一些客户对Pimloc充当数据处理器并使用安全的Redact SaaS解决方案感到高兴-他们管理自己的帐户，上传素材，并可以在编辑和使用之前查看/编辑/更新检测。一些客户在他们的服务器上运行安全的Redact系统，他们既是数据控制器又是处理器，“他指出。
他说：“我们已有超过100名用户签约使用涵盖移动性、娱乐、保险、健康及保安的SaaS服务。我们还在建立一系列内部实施的过程中，“他补充说。
当被问及Pimloc认为未来几年推动其平台增长最快的行业时，他列出了以下几个领域：智能城市/移动平台(安全/分析需求来自议会、零售商、AV等)；保险业，他指出，保险业正在“捕获并使用越来越多的可视数据进行索赔和风险监控”，因此“正在寻找负责任的数据管理和处理系统”；视频/远程医疗，传统咨询转向视频，并推动对视觉诊断的需求；执法方面，安全目标需要得到“默认设计的视觉隐私”的支持(至少在部队受欧洲数据保护法约束的情况下是这样)。
在竞争方面，他指出，初创企业越来越专注于人工智能的专业应用领域-辩称，它们有机会构建令人信服的端到端主张，而大型科技公司更难专注于这些领域。
特别是对于Pimlock，他认为，鉴于“深厚的专业知识”和特定的领域经验，Pimlock在其特定的以安全为重点的利基市场上具有优势。
他辩称：“创造低质量产品的进入门槛很低，但要创造一种足够好的服务，足以在真正的‘野外’镜头中大规模使用，进入门槛很低。”他补充说：“较大科技公司的通才服务与Pimloc/Secure Redact的特定领域条款不匹配。”对于人工智能来说，视频安全镜头是一个困难的领域，针对生活方式/名人或其他一般数据集训练的系统在真正的安全镜头上表现不佳。“。
在一份声明中，Amadeus Capital Partners的医学博士亚历克斯·范·萨默伦(Alex Van Someren)在一份声明中评论了种子资金的问题，他说：“随着视频作为移动、保险、商业和智能城市的数据源不断增长，我们对视频用于远程工作的依赖也在增加，从设计和大规模解决方案来看，对隐私的需求非常迫切。”我们对Pimloc产品迎接这一挑战的潜力感到非常兴奋。“。
世界各地的消费者理所当然地关心企业如何处理24/7全天候捕获的不断增长的视觉数据。我们相信，Pimloc已经开发出一种业界领先的视觉安全和隐私方法，这将使企业和政府能够在保护消费者的同时管理视觉数据的使用。随着他们向更广泛的企业和SaaS市场扩张，我们很高兴能支持他们的愿景。“SpeedInvest公司负责人Rick Hao在另一份支持声明中补充道。
note_en:
The 2016-founded computer vision startup launched a  AI  -powered photo classifier service called  Pholio in 2017 — pitching the service as a way for smartphone users to reclaim agency over their digital memories without having to hand their data over to cloud giants like Google.
 It has since pivoted to position Pholio as a “specialist search and discovery platform” for large image and video collections and live streams (such as those owned by art galleries or broadcasters) — and also launched a second tool powered by its deep learning platform. This product, Secure Redact, offers privacy-focused content moderation tools — enabling its users to find and redact personal data in visual content.
  An example use-case it gives is for law enforcement to anonymize bodycam footage so it can be repurposed for training videos or prepared for submitting as evidence.
 “ Pimloc has been working with diverse image and video content for several years supporting businesses with a host of classification, moderation and data protection challenges (image libraries, art galleries, broadcasters and CCTV providers),” CEO Simon Randall tells TechCrunch.
 “Through our work on the visual privacy side we identified a critical gap in the market for services that allow businesses and governments to manage visual data protection at scale on security footage.  Pimloc has worked in this area for a couple of years building capability and product, as a result  Pimloc has now focussed the business solely around this mission.”
 Secure Redact has two components: A first (automated) step that detects personal data (e.g. faces, heads, bodies) within video content. On top of that is what Randall calls a layer of “intelligent tools” — letting users quickly review and edit results.
 “All detections and tracks are auditable and editable by users prior to accepting and redacting,” he explains, adding: “Personal data extends wider than just faces into other objects and scene content including ID cards, tattoos, phone screens (body worn cameras have a habit of picking up messages on the wearer’s phone screen as they are typing, or sensitive notes on their laptop or notebook).”
 One specific user of redaction the tool he mentions is the University of Bristol. There a research group, led by Dr Dima Damen, an associate professor in computer vision, is participating in an international consortium of 12 universities which is aiming to amass the largest dataset on egocentric vision — and needs to be able to anonymise the video data set before making it available for academic/open source use.
 On the legal side, Randall says  Pimloc   offers a range of data processing models — thereby catering to differences in how/where data can be processed. “Some customers are happy for Pimloc to act as data processor and use the Secure Redact SaaS solution — they manage their account, they upload footage, and can review/edit/update detections prior to redaction and usage. Some customers run the Secure Redact system on their servers where they are both data controller and processor,” he notes.
 “We have over 100 users signed up for the SaaS service covering mobility, entertainment, insurance, health and security. We are also in the process of setting up a host of on-premise implementations,” he adds.
 Asked which sectors Pimloc sees driving the most growth for its platform in the coming years, he lists the following: smart cities/mobility platforms (with safety/analytics demand coming from the likes of councils, retailers, AVs); the insurance industry, which he notes is “capturing and using an increasing amount of visual data for claims and risk monitoring” and thus “looking at responsible systems for data management and processing”; video/telehealth, with traditional consultations moving into video and driving demand for visual diagnosis; and law enforcement, where security goals need to be supported by “visual privacy designed in by default” (at least where forces are subject to European data protection law).
 On the competitive front, he notes that startups are increasingly focusing on specialist application areas for AI — arguing they have an opportunity to build compelling end-to-end propositions which are harder for larger tech companies to focus on.
 For Pimlock specifically he argues it has an edge in its particular security-focused niche — given  “deep expertise” and specific domain experience.
 “There are low barriers to entry to create a low quality product but very high technical barriers to create a service that is good enough to use at scale with real ‘in the wild’ footage,” he argues, adding:  “The generalist services of the larger tech players do not match-up with domain specific provisions of Pimloc/Secure Redact. Video security footage is a difficult domain for AI, systems trained on lifestyle/celebrity or other general data sets perform poorly on real security footage.”
 Commenting on the seed funding in a statement, Alex van Someren, MD of  Amadeus Capital Partners,   said: “There is a critical need for privacy by design and large-scale solutions, as video grows as a data source for mobility, insurance, commerce and smart cities, while our reliance on video for remote working increases. We are very excited about the potential of  Pimloc’s products to meet this challenge.”
 “Consumers around the world are rightfully concerned with how enterprises are handling the growing volume of visual data being captured 24/7. We believe  Pimloc has developed an industry leading approach to visual security and privacy that will allow businesses and governments to manage the usage of visual data whilst protecting consumers. We are excited to support their vision as they expand into the wider Enterprise and SaaS markets,” added Rick Hao, principal at Speedinvest, in another supporting statement.
title: Pimloc从其基于AI的视觉搜索和密文工具中获得180万美元
title_en: Pimloc gets $1.8M for its AI-based visual search and redaction tool

note:
TikTok正在对QAnon阴谋论采取强硬立场，将禁令扩大到所有宣传极右翼在线运动提出毫无根据的想法的视频的内容或账户。
这一行动加强了视频分享应用Qanon之前针对Qanon的执法，Qanon的支持者曾利用该应用上的特定标签来散布毫无根据的理论。现在，在TikTok上分享QAnon相关内容的用户将从应用程序中删除他们的账户。
TikTok的一位发言人在给NPR的一份声明中表示：推广Qanon的内容和账户违反了我们的虚假信息政策，我们将它们从我们的平台上删除。我们还采取了重大措施，通过将相关术语重定向到我们的社区指南，使这些内容更难在搜索和标签中找到。
就在TikTok对QAnon采取全面行动的同时，Facebook、Twitter、YouTube和其他科技巨头已经宣布禁止发布支持特朗普的阴谋论内容。QAnon成立于2017年10月，在很大程度上要归功于社交媒体公司，并在网上积累了大量追随者。
自由派非营利性监督组织Media Matters for America的总裁安吉洛·卡鲁松(Angelo Carusone)表示，应该承认一件好事和重要的事情，即使它早就应该得到承认。TikTok正在认识到，根据Qanon运动的性质，你不能仅仅摆脱他们的社区，内容本身就是问题所在。
本月早些时候，Media Matters发现了十多个TikTokkers标签，这些标签被用来传播关于特朗普总统冠状病毒检测呈阳性的QAnon阴谋论，关于民主党总统候选人乔·拜登的错误信念，以及质疑疫情现实的视频。
卡鲁松说：我们谈论的视频浏览量仅针对我们确定的QAnon社区的一小部分，就有数亿次。
TikTok在美国拥有1亿月度活跃用户，它在给Media Matters的一份声明中悄悄扩大了对Qanon的禁令，但几乎没有引起关注。周六，TikTok的一位发言人向NPR证实了这一政策。
加州大学伯克利分校(UC Berkeley)计算机科学教授、TikTok外部内容审核专家委员会成员汉尼·法里德(Hany Farid)表示，对于如何在不放大潜在理论的情况下对错误信息做出回应，社交网络内部存在紧张情绪。
当你禁止它的时候，你就给了它可信度。法里德告诉NPR，你要注意这件事。
但这场运动变得足够大，足够危险，以至于人们看着风景说，是的，这完全失控了，他说。他们做这件事很慢吗？可能吧。但当平台行动太快时，就会受到批评。所以这里有一个进退两难的境地。
TikTok混合使用人工智能和数以千计的人类内容版主，试图遏制令人不安的内容。这款中国人拥有的应用程序最出名的是病毒式舞蹈挑战和喜剧表演。
根据TikTok；的社区指南，网站上禁止发布对个人、我们的社区或更大范围的公众造成伤害的错误信息，包括医疗错误信息，Qanon通过宣扬关于这种致命冠状病毒的错误观念来从事这些错误信息。
Media Matters的Carusone表示，TikTok上的错误信息账户一直很聪明，通过劫持原本无害的标签，或者创建略微用代码编写的新标签，以及其他规避遏制内容的努力的策略，来避免被发现。
卡鲁松说，这项政策的考验将是它对TikTok上新Qanon内容的创建和萌芽有多大影响。如果你知道你的视频在它有机会传播之前就会被淘汰，你就不太可能花时间污染TikTok池了。
TikTok在美国的未来仍然不确定。上个月，一名联邦法官暂时阻止了特朗普政府关闭这款应用的尝试。但白宫要求TikTok从北京所有者手中剥离或停止运营的另一项命令仍在执行，TikTok寻找美国买家或关闭美国业务的最后期限是11月12日。
特朗普官员提到了对TikTok中国企业主字节跳动(ByteDance)的国家安全担忧，但TikTok长期以来一直对这一努力不屑一顾，认为这是为了获得政治积分。该公司表示，美国用户数据由一个以美国为首的团队控制，中国政府从未要求访问这些数据。
note_en:
TikTok is toughening its stance against the QAnon conspiracy theory, expanding its ban to all content or accounts that promote videos advancing baseless ideas from the far-right online movement.
  The action hardens the video-sharing app's  previous enforcement against QAnon that targeted specific hashtags on the app that QAnon supporters have used to spread unfounded theories. Now, users that share QAnon-related content on TikTok will have their accounts deleted from the app.
  "Content and accounts that promote QAnon violate our disinformation policy and we remove them from our platform," a TikTok spokesperson said in a statement to NPR. "We've also taken significant steps to make this content harder to find across search and hashtags by redirecting associated terms to our Community Guidelines."
  TikTok's sweeping action against QAnon comes just as Facebook, Twitter, YouTube and other technology giants have announced bans on content from the Trump-supporting conspiracy theory. QAnon  started in October 2017 and has amassed an enormous following online thanks largely to social media companies.
  "There should be recognition of a thing that is good and significant, even if it's long overdue," said Angelo Carusone, president of the liberal nonprofit watchdog group Media Matters for America. "TikTok is recognizing that by the nature of the QAnon movement, you can't just get rid of their communities, the content itself is the problem."
       Earlier this month, Media Matters  identified more than a dozen hashtags TikTokkers used to spread QAnon conspiracy theories about President Trump's positive coronavirus test, false beliefs about Democratic presidential candidate Joe Biden and videos questioning the reality of the pandemic.
  "We're talking about hundreds of millions of video views just for a limited segment of QAnon communities that we identified," Carusone said.
  TikTok, which has 100 million monthly active users in the U.S., made its expanded ban against QAnon quietly in a statement to Media Matters, where it garnered little attention. A TikTok spokesperson confirmed the policy to NPR on Saturday.
  Hany Farid, a UC Berkeley computer science professor who is a member of TikTok's committee of outside content moderation experts, said there is tension within social networks over how to respond to misinformation without also amplifying the underlying theories.
     "When you ban it, you give it credibility. You give it attention," Farid told NPR.
  "But the movement got big enough and dangerous enough that people were looking at the landscape and saying, 'Yeah, this is completely out of control,' " he said. "Were they slow to do it? Probably. But platforms get criticized when they act too quickly. So there is a dilemma there."
  TikTok uses a mix of artificial intelligence and thousands of human content moderators to try to curb troubling content. The Chinese-owned app is best-known for viral dance challenges and comedic performances.
     According to TikTok's  Community Guidelines, misinformation that "causes harm to individuals, our community or the larger public" is prohibited on the site, including medical misinformation, which QAnon has engaged in by pushing false notions about the deadly coronavirus.
  Carusone of Media Matters said misinformation accounts on TikTok have been clever about avoiding detection by hijacking otherwise-benign hashtags, or creating new hashtags that are written slightly in code, among other strategies to evade efforts to curb the content.
  "The test of this policy will be how much it affects the creation and germination of new QAnon content on TikTok," Carusone said. "If you know your video is going to be eliminated before it has a chance to spread, you're less likely to spend time polluting the TikTok pool."
  The future of TikTok in the U.S. remains uncertain. A federal judge last month  temporarily halted a Trump administration attempt to shut down the app. But a separate order from the White House for TikTok to divest from its Beijing owner or cease operations remains in place, with a deadline of Nov. 12 for TikTok to find an American buyer or close down its U.S. operations.
  Trump officials  cite national security concerns with TikTok's China-based corporate owner, ByteDance, but TikTok has  long dismissed the effort as an crusade to score political points. The company says U.S. user data is controlled by an American-led team and that the Chinese government has never requested access to the data.
title: TikTok将QAnon禁令扩展到标签之外，删除了所有宣传推进阴谋的视频的内容和账户
title_en: TikTok expands its QAnon bans beyond hashtags, removing all content and accounts that promote videos advancing the conspiracy

2018年11月，在历时41年的史诗般的航行之后，旅行者2号终于越过了标志着太阳影响极限的边界，进入了星际空间。但是，这个小探测器的任务还没有完成--它现在正将太阳系以外太空的信息发回家。
它揭示了一些令人惊讶的事情。随着旅行者2号距离太阳越来越远，空间密度也在增加。
这已经不是第一次检测到这种密度增加了。2012年进入星际空间的旅行者1号在另一个单独的位置探测到了类似的密度梯度。
旅行者2号的新数据表明，旅行者1号的探测不仅是合法的，而且密度的增加可能是非常局部星际介质(VLIM)的一个大范围特征。
太阳系的边缘可以由几个不同的边界来定义，但旅行者号探测器穿过的边界被称为日顶层，它是由太阳风来定义的。
这是一种持续不断的超音速电离等离子体风，从太阳的各个方向涌出，而太阳顶层是这样一个点，在这个点上，这种风的向外压力不再足够强，无法从星际空间推入风中。
太阳顶层内的空间是日光层，它外面的空间是VLIM。但是日光层并不是一个圆形的球体。它更像一个椭圆形，一端是太阳系，后面是一条飘动的尾巴；鼻子指向太阳系在银河系的轨道方向。
两架航行者都在机头穿过了太阳顶层，但在日经纬度上相差67度，在经度上相差43度。
太空通常被认为是真空，但事实并非如此，并非完全如此。物质的密度极低，但它仍然存在。在太阳系中，太阳风的质子和电子密度平均为每立方厘米3到10个粒子，但离太阳越远，它的密度就越低。
据计算，银河系中星际介质的平均电子密度约为每立方厘米0.037个粒子。外层日光层的等离子体密度约为每立方厘米0.002个电子。
当旅行者号探测器越过太阳顶层时，他们的等离子体波科学仪器通过等离子体振荡探测到等离子体的电子密度。
旅行者1号于2012年8月25日越过太阳顶层，距离地球121.6个天文单位(这是地球与太阳之间距离的121.6倍，约合181亿公里)。
当旅行者1号于2013年10月23日在距离122.6 天文单位(183亿公里)处穿越日层顶后首次测量到等离子体振荡时，它探测到的等离子体密度为每立方厘米0.055个电子。
旅行者2号绕着木星、土星、天王星和海王星飞行，绕了很长一段路，于2018年11月5日在119个天文单位(178亿公里)的距离越过了日顶。它在119.7个天文单位(179亿)的距离上测量了2019年1月30日的等离子体振荡，发现等离子体密度为每立方厘米0.039个电子，非常接近旅行者1号的测量结果。
两种仪器都报告密度增加。旅行者1号在太空中又旅行了20个天文单位(29亿公里)后，报告说每立方厘米大约有0.13个电子。
但旅行者2号在2019年6月进行的探测显示，在距离124.2个天文单位(185亿个单位)的地方，密度的增加幅度要大得多，达到每立方厘米约0.12个电子。
考虑到地球大气压下的等离子体的电子密度为每立方厘米10^13，这些数量可能看起来很小，但它们足够重要，足以引起我们的兴趣--特别是因为目前还不清楚是什么原因造成了它们。
一种理论认为，当星际磁力线覆盖在日顶时，它们会变得更强。这可能会产生电磁离子回旋不稳定性，耗尽复盖区的等离子体。旅行者2号在穿越日顶时确实探测到了比预期更强的磁场。
另一种理论是，被星际风吹来的物质在到达日顶时应该会减速，从而造成某种交通堵塞。外太阳系探测器“新地平线”可能已经探测到了这一点，该探测器在2018年探测到了由太阳顶层中性氢积聚而产生的微弱紫外光。
也有可能这两种解释都起到了作用。当两个旅行者探测器继续他们的星际空间之旅时，他们未来所做的测量可能会帮助弄清楚这一点。但这可能是一个很长的赌注。
研究人员在他们的论文中写道，目前还不能确定旅行者号是否能够在足够远的距离上操作，以区分这两类型号。&#34；研究人员在他们的论文中写道，旅行者号是否能够在足够远的距离上操作，以区分这两类型号，这一点并不确定。
note_en:
In November 2018, after an epic, 41-year voyage, Voyager 2 finally crossed the boundary that marked the limit of the Sun's influence and entered interstellar space. But the little probe's mission isn't done yet - it's now sending home information about the space beyond the Solar System.
   And it's revealing something surprising. As Voyager 2 moves farther and farther from the Sun, the  density of space is increasing.
 It's not the first time this density increase has been detected. Voyager 1, which entered interstellar space in 2012, detected a similar density gradient at a separate location.
 Voyager 2's new data show that not only was Voyager 1's detection legit, but that the increase in density may be a large-scale feature of the very local interstellar medium (VLIM).
 The Solar System's edge can be defined by a few different boundaries, but the one crossed by the Voyager probes is known as the heliopause, and it's defined by the solar wind.
 This is a constant supersonic wind of ionised plasma that streams out from the Sun in all directions, and the heliopause is the point at which the outward pressure of that wind is no longer strong enough to push into the wind from interstellar space.
  The space inside the heliopause is the heliosphere, and the space outside it is the VLIM. But the heliosphere isn't a round sphere. It's more like an oval, with the Solar System at one end, and a streaming tail behind; the "nose" is pointed in the  direction of the Solar System's orbit in the Milky Way.
 Both Voyagers crossed the heliopause at the nose, but with a difference of 67 degrees in heliographic latitude and 43 degrees difference longitude.
   Space is generally thought of as a vacuum, but it isn't, not completely. The density of matter is extremely low, but it still exists. In the Solar System, the solar wind has an average proton and electron density of  3 to 10 particles per cubic centimetre, but it grows lower the farther out you go from the Sun.
 The mean electron density of the interstellar medium in the Milky Way, out among the stars,  has been calculated to be around 0.037 particles per cubic centimetre. And the plasma density in the outer heliosphere is around 0.002 electrons per cubic centimetre.
 As the Voyager probes crossed beyond the heliopause, their Plasma Wave Science instruments detected the electron density of the plasma through plasma oscillations.
 Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause on 25 August 2012, at a distance of 121.6 astronomical units from Earth (that's 121.6 times the distance between the Earth and Sun, so roughly 18.1 billion km).
 When it first measured the plasma oscillations after crossing the heliopause on 23 October 2013 at a distance of 122.6 astronomical units (18.3 billion km), Voyager 1 detected a plasma density of  0.055 electrons per cubic centimetres.
   Voyager 2, which took the long way around, flying by Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune, crossed the heliopause on 5 November 2018 at a distance of 119 astronomical units (17.8 billion km). It measured the plasma oscillations on 30 January 2019 at a distance of 119.7 astronomical units (17.9 billion), finding a plasma density of 0.039 electrons per cubic centimetre, very close to the Voyager 1 measurement.
 And both instruments reported an increase in density. After travelling another 20 astronomical units (2.9 billion km) through space, Voyager 1 reported an increase to about 0.13 electrons per cubic centimetre.
 But detections made by Voyager 2 in June 2019 showed a much sharper increase in density to about 0.12 electrons per cubic centimetre, at a distance of 124.2 astronomical units (18.5 billion units).
 Given that  plasma at Earth's atmospheric pressure has an electron density of 10^13 per cubic centimetre, those amounts may seem tiny, but they're significant enough to warrant our interest - especially since it's not clear what causes them.
 One theory is that the interstellar magnetic field lines become stronger as they drape over the heliopause. This could generate an electromagnetic ion cyclotron instability that depletes the plasma from the draping region. Voyager 2 did detect  a stronger magnetic field than expected when it crossed the heliopause.
 Another theory is that material blown by the interstellar wind should slow as it reaches the heliopause, causing a sort of traffic jam. This has possibly been detected by outer Solar System probe New Horizons,  which in 2018 picked up the faint ultraviolet glow resulting from a buildup of neutral hydrogen at the heliopause.
 It's also possible that both explanations play a role. Future measurements taken by both Voyager probes as they continue their journey out into interstellar space could help figure it out. But that might be a long bet to take.
 "It is not certain," the researchers wrote in their paper, "whether the Voyagers will be able to operate far enough to distinguish between these two classes of models."
title: 旅行者探测到太阳系外空间密度的增加
title_en: Voyagers Detect an Increase in the Density of Space Outside the Solar System

根据公告，已经推出了TV，这是一个免费的24小时流行音乐视频直播，其中还将包括“独家新音乐视频和首映式，特别策划的音乐视频块，现场表演和活动，以及排行榜倒计时和嘉宾”。
Apple Music TV只能在Apple Music应用程序和Apple TV应用程序上向美国居民提供。它可以在apple.co/AppleMusicTV上找到，也可以在Apple Music和Apple TV应用程序的浏览选项卡中找到。
该服务于周一上午(10月19日)在Apple Music上首播，并对美国有史以来最受欢迎的100首歌曲进行了倒计时。周四(10月22日)，它将庆祝布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀(Bruce Springsteens)即将发行的“Letter to You”专辑，活动包括“全天布鲁斯接管”(All Day Bruce Takeover)，其中包括他最受欢迎的视频的音乐视频块，苹果音乐电台主播赞恩·洛(Zane Lowe)的采访，以及一场特别的直播粉丝活动。
它还将于周五下午12点举行两场独家视频首映式。美国东部时间/上午9点。PT、Joji的《777》、Saint JHN的《华丽》；届时频道每周五首播新视频。
在推出之前，更多的细节尚不清楚，但洛的参与意味着新的电视频道与苹果音乐1电台(前身为Beats 1)、其直播主持人以及其职权范围之间的联系。Apple Music 1电台面向热门节目，但以冒险节目为特色，主要由洛负责监督。然而，该频道与斯普林斯汀这样的遗产艺术家一起推出的事实意味着范围上的不同。
此举是多年来人们期待已久的猜测和讨论的高潮，人们一直在猜测和谈论该公司将何时以及如何进军音乐视频领域，该领域长期以来一直由YouTube、Vevo和过去的MTV主导。
令人惊讶的是，这份声明中没有具体提到音乐纪录片，这是该公司已经投资的一种格式-最引人注目的是即将上映的比莉·艾利什(Billie Eilish)长篇纪录片“世界有一点模糊”(The World‘s A Little Fuzzy)，该纪录片计划于2月份上映。
苹果音乐自2015年正式推出以来，已经创造了大量原创内容，包括演唱会电影、采访等--声明承诺：“这些内容现在也将在苹果音乐电视上占有一席之地。”
note_en:
has launched   TV, a free 24-hour curated livestream of popular music videos that will also include “exclusive new music videos and premiers, special curated music video blocks, and live shows and events as well as chart countdowns and guests,” according to the announcement.
 Apple Music TV will be available to U.S. residents only on the Apple Music app and the Apple TV app. It can be found at   apple.co/AppleMusicTV and  in the browse tab in the Apple Music and Apple TV app.
 The service premiered Monday morning (Oct. 19) with a countdown of the top 100 all-time most-streamed songs in the U.S. on Apple Music. On Thursday (October 22), it will celebrate the upcoming release of Bruce Springsteens’s “Letter to You” album with an “all day Bruce takeover” featuring music-video blocks of his most popular videos, an interview with Zane Lowe, anchor of Apple Music’s radio station, and a special livestream fan event.
  It will also have two exclusive video premieres on Friday at 12 p.m. ET/9 a.m. PT, Joji’s “777” and Saint Jhn’s “Gorgeous”; the channel will premiere new videos every Friday at that time.
 Further details were scant before launch, but Lowe’s involvement implies a connection between the new TV channel and Apple Music 1 radio station (formerly called Beats 1), its on-air hosts and its purview, which is geared toward hits but features adventurous programming, largely overseen by Lowe. However, the fact that the channel is launching with a heritage artist like Springsteen implies a difference in scope.
 The move is the long-anticipated culmination of years of speculation and talk about when and how the company would move into the music video space, which has long been dominated by YouTube, Vevo and, in the past, MTV.
 Surprisingly, the announcement includes no specific mention of music documentaries, which is a format the company has already invested in — most notably the forthcoming Billie Eilish feature-length doc “The World’s a Little Blurry,” which is scheduled for release in February.
 Apple Music has created a bounty of original content since its official launch in 2015, including concert films, interviews and more — “that content will now also have a home on Apple Music TV,” the announcement promises.
title: 苹果推出Apple Music TV，这是一个免费的24小时流行音乐视频直播，在美国可以通过苹果的音乐和电视应用程序获得
title_en: Apple launches Apple Music TV, a free 24-hour curated livestream of popular music videos, available in the US on Apple's Music and TV apps

Facebook正在扩展其Messenger API，以支持Instagram消息传递，使开发人员和企业能够将Instagram消息传递集成到其他商业应用程序中。
该公司补充说，新的Messenger API现在处于封闭测试版，将首次支持Instagram上的自动响应。该公司早在2016年就首次向机器人开放了Messenger平台，现在它正寻求通过让企业预先配置对一些最常见的入站查询的自动响应来加快Instagram上的响应速度。
在过去的几年里，Facebook一直在推动合并其各种资产，包括Messenger、WhatsApp和Instagram。上个月，该公司宣布这一运动中的一个重要里程碑，开始允许Messenger和Instagram用户相互传递信息。虽然Messenger API本身不支持跨应用通信，但就其作为商业通信平台的效用而言，这一最新扩展将使Instagram与Messenger处于更加平等的地位。
自从Facebook在2012年斥资10亿美元收购Instagram以来，Instagram已经走过了很长一段路，而且它拥有超过10亿用户，对于希望增加参与度和销售额的企业来说，这是一个有吸引力的提议。事实上，Instagram长期以来一直为企业提供无数工具，并凭借其自身的能力成为一个电子商务平台，消费者可以在这里发现并从零售商那里购买商品。
通过向Instagram开放Messenger API，这将允许企业从他们日常工作流程中使用的任何应用程序(如客户关系管理(CRM)系统)中访问客户的对话。例如，这意味着来自客户的消息可以与他们的订单历史记录一起显示。
升级后的Messenger API目前作为测试版计划的一部分，面向特定的开发商和企业提供，如阿迪达斯、Amaro、Glossier、H&amp；M、Michael Kors、Nars和Sephora。
音频问题：了解基于云的新API解决方案如何解决视频会议中不完美、令人沮丧的音频问题。访问此处
note_en:
Facebook is  expanding its Messenger API to support Instagram messaging, enabling developers and businesses to integrate messaging from Instagram into other business applications.
 The company added that the new Messenger API, available in closed beta now, will support automated responses for the first time on Instagram. The company first opened the Messenger platform to bots  back in 2016, and now it’s looking to expedite response rates on Instagram by letting businesses pre-configure automatic responses to some of their most common inbound enquiries.
   Facebook  has been pushing to unite its various properties including Messenger, WhatsApp, and Instagram over the past couple of years, and last month the company announced a major milestone in this movement when it  started making it possible for Messenger and Instagram users to message each other. While the Messenger API itself doesn’t support cross-app communication, this latest expansion will bring Instagram onto a more equal footing with its Messenger counterpart in terms of its utility as a business communications platform.
  Instagram has come a long way since Facebook  swooped in with $1 billion to buy the photo-sharing app back in 2012, and  with well over 1 billion users it is an attractive proposition for businesses looking to increase their engagement and sales. Indeed, Instagram has long  offered myriad tools for businesses, and has emerged as an ecommerce platform in its own right  where consumers can discover and buy goods from retailers.
 By opening the Messenger API to Instagram, this will let businesses access its customers’ conversations from within whatever applications they use as part of their daily workflow, such as customer relationship management (CRM) systems. This means that messages from a customer could be displayed alongside their order history, for example.
 The upgraded Messenger API is currently available as part of a beta program with select developers and businesses, such as Adidas, Amaro, Glossier, H&M, Michael Kors, Nars, and Sephora.
 The audio problem:Learn how new cloud-based API solutions are solving imperfect, frustrating audio in video conferences.  Access here
title: Facebook将Messenger API扩展到Instagram，允许开发人员和企业将Instagram的消息集成到其他应用程序中
title_en: Facebook expands its Messenger API to Instagram, letting developers and businesses integrate messaging from Instagram into other apps

东京(路透社)-日本反垄断监管机构新任负责人周一表示，日本将与美国和欧洲联手，打击四大科技公司的任何市场滥用行为，这是东京将加入全球监管数字平台运营商的努力的迹象。
日本公平贸易委员会(FTC)主席古屋和之(Kazuyuki Furuya)也表示，如果这类交易的规模足够大，东京方面可能会对涉及健身跟踪器制造商Fitbit FIT.N的任何合并或商业合并展开调查。
他对路透表示，“如果任何合并或企业结盟的规模很大，我们可以对买方收购初创企业(如Fitbit)的过程展开反垄断调查.”“我们正密切关注包括欧洲在内的事态发展。”
欧盟反垄断监管机构在8月份对Alphabet GOOGL.O子公司谷歌(Google)以21亿美元收购Fitbit的交易展开调查，该交易旨在与苹果(Apple AAPL.O)和三星(Samsung)005930.KS在可穿戴技术市场展开竞争。
日本正在为规范平台运营商奠定基础。其中包括谷歌(Google)、苹果(Apple)、亚马逊(Amazon AMZN.O)和Facebook FB.O等被称为Gafa&34；的大型科技巨头，它们在西方国家面临各种反垄断调查。
古屋说，像Gafa这样的跨国公司在全球都有类似的商业做法，这使得全球协调至关重要。
他说：“我们将与美国和欧洲同行密切合作，如果有任何妨碍竞争的举动，我们都会做出回应。”
“这是一个我将积极推进的领域，”他表示，并补充称，如果数字平台滥用其主导市场地位侵害消费者，联邦贸易委员会已准备好展开调查。
古屋于9月上任，他还表示，联邦贸易委员会将对日本手机市场进行研究，看看是否有任何改善的空间来刺激竞争。
任何此类举措都将有助于首相菅义伟(Yoshiide Suga)大幅削减日本手机资费的努力，他一再批评手机资费过高。
古屋反驳了这样的观点，即帮助政府实现其政策优先事项可能会削弱联邦贸易委员会作为一个受权独立于政治干预采取行动的机构的地位。
古屋说：“如果政府有一个政策重点，联邦贸易委员会无疑应该考虑它在这方面能做些什么。”
“通过参与政府对政策问题的辩论，我们一直在反映我们在这个过程中的想法。这是我们组织应该做的事情。“
note_en:
TOKYO (Reuters) - Japan will join forces with the United States and Europe to take on any market abuses by the four Big Tech companies, the new head of its antitrust watchdog said on Monday, a sign Tokyo will join global efforts to regulate digital platform operators.
  Kazuyuki Furuya, chairman of Japan's Fair Trade Commission (FTC), also said Tokyo could open a probe into any merger or business tie-up involving fitness tracker maker Fitbit   FIT.N  if the size of such deals are big enough.
 “If the size of any merger or business-tie up is big, we can launch an anti-monopoly investigation into the buyer’s process of acquiring a start-up (like Fitbit),” he told Reuters. “We’re closely watching developments including in Europe.”
 EU antitrust regulators in August launched an investigation into a $2.1 billion deal by Alphabet   GOOGL.O  unit Google's bid to buy Fitbit that aimed to take on Apple   AAPL.O  and Samsung   005930.KS  in the wearable technology market.
 Japan is laying the groundwork to regulate platform operators. Among them are big tech giants dubbed "GAFA" - Google, Apple, Amazon   AMZN.O  and Facebook   FB.O  - that face various antitrust probes in western nations.
  Multi-national companies like GAFA have similar business practices across the globe, which makes global coordination crucial, Furuya said.
 “We’ll work closely with our U.S. and European counterparts, and respond if to any moves that hamper competition,” he said.
 “This is an area I will push through aggressively,” he said, adding the FTC was ready to open probes if digital platformers abuse their dominant market positions against consumers.
 Furuya, who assumed the post in September, also said the FTC would conduct research into Japan’s mobile phone market to see whether there is any room for improvement to spur competition.
  Any such move would help Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga’s push to slash Japan’s mobile phone charges, which he has repeatedly criticised as too high.
 Furuya countered the view that helping the government meet its policy priorities could undermine the FTC’s position as a body mandated to act independently from political meddling.
 “If there’s a policy priority for the government, there’s no doubt the FTC should think about what it can do on that front,” Furuya said.
 “By participating in the government’s debate on policy issues, we have been reflecting our thinking in the process. This is something our organisation should do.”
title: 日本公平贸易委员会表示，该国将加入美国和欧洲的行列，挑战谷歌、苹果、Facebook和亚马逊的市场滥用行为
title_en: Japan's Fair Trade Commission says the country will join US and Europe in challenging market abuses by Google, Apple, Facebook, and Amazon

商业·公开安大略省的一位寡妇与苹果就她已经合法拥有的在线内容陷入了长达四年的斗争。
多伦多的卡罗尔·安妮·诺布尔(Carol Anne Noble)想要访问她和丈夫共享的苹果账户-但用的是他的名字-这样她就可以履行她在他去世前对他做出的承诺。
但是，这家科技巨头没有给她密码，而是要求她跳过复杂而昂贵的法律程序，以满足专家所说的过时的美国法律。
这对我来说是一记真正的耳光，诺布尔说，她结婚41年，是丈夫遗产的遗嘱执行人和唯一受益人。唐·诺布尔于2016年末死于一种罕见的脊椎癌。
基本上，他们会希望法院下令把我丈夫拥有的东西给我，而这些东西已经传给了我……。66岁的诺布尔在接受“公开”采访时说，这非常奇怪。
专家表示，科技公司拒绝交出数字资产是一个问题，影响到从股票、保险单和贝宝(PayPal)到游戏信用、社交媒体帖子和全家福等方方面面。
在线资产专家、阿尔伯塔大学(University Of Alberta)荣休教授彼得·罗恩(Peter Lown)告诉Go Public，这是一个只会变得更大的大问题，这让加拿大人任由美国公司及其规则摆布。
诺布尔的丈夫在生命的最后六个月里一直躺在床上，与一种名为脊索瘤的慢性病作斗争。他会花几个小时用苹果设备记录疾病的发展过程。
他希望把所有这些都写进一本书里，送给他的家人，但诺布尔说，最终很明显，他活不了那么久了。
唐去世前几天，我不得不送他去医院…。那天晚上他对我说的最后一句话是，你得写这本书。不久之后，他就陷入了昏迷，她说。
2017年初，当来宝集团联系苹果时，她被告知，她所需要做的就是证明她拥有丈夫遗产的合法权利。她寄出了公司要求的所有文件，包括他的死亡证明和指定她为遗嘱执行人的遗嘱。
他们给我回电话说，不，这还不够--你必须有法院命令(但)必须付钱才能下达法院命令，她说。
法院命令的费用从大约2000美元到数万美元不等-这取决于他们是否反对。
来宝集团在与苹果公司来回奔波多年后，出于绝望，在今年早些时候聘请了一名律师来帮助引导公司的需求。
诺布尔的律师迪拉吉·辛德瓦尼(Dheeraj Sindhwani)在安大略省从事家庭法业务，他说，这是一个棘手的情况。
数字资产是由一家私人持股公司管理的东西之一……但它不像银行账户，我们不能进去说，这里有死亡证明，这里有遗嘱认证文件，我们可以进入这里获取访问权限。(注：数字资产是由私人持股的公司管理的。但它不像银行账户，我们不能进去说，这里有死亡证明，这里有遗嘱认证文件。)
洛恩说，他见过涉及谷歌、Facebook和其他公司的类似案例，科技公司经常对加拿大用户不屑一顾，认为这是一种滋扰。
所以人们被束缚住了。即使他们通过了一些，还会有更多，他说。
苹果拒绝了“上市”的采访请求，也没有回应我们关于来宝的情况及其数字资产政策的具体问题。
该公司告诉来宝，提供密码将违反1986年制定的一项美国法律--《电子通信隐私法》(Electronic Communications Privacy Act)。
这项法律旨在保护美国人免受不必要的政府和警察监视，禁止公司提供个人电子信息。
但洛恩表示，这项已有数十年历史的法律并不适用于今天社交媒体和其他数字资产的高度在线世界。
[然而]服务提供商真心地使用它&#34；[然而]Content…。所以他们可以说，对不起，我不能向你透露这件事，因为那会违反我在法案下的义务。
那些制定了专门针对数字资产的法律的国家(包括欧盟)的公民不会遇到同样的问题。对于这些人，苹果服务的条款和条件授予您通常居住地的法律和法院管辖权。
一些加拿大人已经做到了这一点。2014年，莫琳·亨利的成年儿子的身体在多伦多的一个码头被发现-疑似自杀-之后，她想访问他的数字账户，看看他们是否会给她任何答案。
她得到了法院的命令，先是来自安大略省，后来又来自加州北部的一家法院。苹果和贝尔遵守了安大略省的命令，谷歌加拿大公司对此进行了斗争，但在亨利获得美国订单后给了她数据。她仍在法庭上与Facebook抗争。
然而，Lown说，更多情况下，加拿大人受到美国法律的约束，尽管保护像亨利和诺布尔这样的人的拟议立法已经起草完毕，并准备通过。
2016年，一个由律师、法官和学者组成的独立小组提出了一项修改省级继承法的提议，即通过受托人统一获取数字资产法案，洛恩是该小组的成员。
它将为加拿大人解决这类问题，但到目前为止，只有萨斯喀彻温省使其成为法律。
这将迫使这些大型科技公司解决这个问题，因为就目前的情况而言，祝你好运，试图联系到这些公司中几乎总是总部设在加利福尼亚州的任何人。
罗恩说，只有当加拿大拥有这种杠杆作用时，科技公司才会迎合加拿大人的需求。
来宝表示，当用户注册该服务时，苹果有责任透明和坦率地对待其政策，而不是将这些信息印在小字上。
她还希望看到省级和地区政府在解决这个问题上做得更好。
&#34；这是一个需要立即解决的领域…。她说，人们能够接触到他们所爱的人的临终照片和各种东西，这一点非常重要。
在Go Public与苹果联系后，苹果与来宝进行了接触，提供了法院命令需要包括的细节。
对于固定收入，她认为自己负担不起，并准备放弃，直到她的律师Sindhwani提出无偿完成申请。
我们讲述你们的故事，揭露不法行为，并追究权力的责任。

note_en:
Business  · GO PUBLIC      An Ontario widow is locked in a four-year battle with Apple over online material she already legally owns.
  Carol Anne Noble of Toronto wants access to an Apple account she and her husband shared — but was under his name — so she can fulfil a promise she made to him before he died.
   But instead of giving her the password she's forgotten, the tech giant is demanding she jump through complicated and expensive legal hoops to satisfy what experts say is an outdated American law.
  "It's a real slap in the face to me," said Noble who, after 41 years of marriage, was the executor and sole beneficiary of her husband's estate. Don Noble died from a rare spinal cancer in late 2016.
  "Basically, they want an order from the court to give me what my husband owned and it is already bequeathed to me... it's very strange," Noble, 66, told Go Public.
    Experts say that tech companies refusing to hand over digital assets is a problem affecting everything from stocks, insurance policies and PayPal to gaming credits, social media posts and family photos.
  It's a "big" problem that will "only get bigger," Peter Lown, online assets expert and professor emeritus at the University of Alberta, told Go Public, leaving Canadians at the mercy of the U.S.-based companies and their rules.
    Noble's husband spent the last six months of his life confined to a bed as he battled a slow-moving form of cancer called chordoma. He'd spend hours chronicling the progression of the disease using Apple devices.
  He was hoping to put it all together in a book for his family, but Noble says it eventually became clear he wouldn't live long enough.
  "A few days before Don died I had to take him to the hospital … His last words to me that night were 'You will have to write the book.' He went into a coma shortly after," she said.
      When Noble contacted Apple in early 2017, she was told all she needed to do was prove she has the legal right to her husband's estate. She sent all the documents the company asked for, including his death certificate and the will designating her as executor.
  "They called me back and said, no, that's not good enough — you have to have a court order [but] having to pay to put in a court order is a huge deal," she said.
  Court orders can cost from about $2,000 to tens of thousands of dollars — depending on whether they're opposed.
  After going back and forth with Apple for years, Noble, out of desperation, hired a lawyer earlier this year to help navigate the company's demands.
  "It's a tricky situation," said Dheeraj Sindhwani, Noble's lawyer, who practises family law in Ontario.
  "Digital assets are one of those things where it's being governed by a privately held corporation... it's not like a bank account where we can go in and say, here's a death certificate, here's a probate document and get access."
  Lown says he has seen similar  cases involving Google, Facebook and others, and that tech companies often "brush off" Canadian users "as a nuisance."
  "So people are getting hooped by the hoops. Even if they get through some, there will be more," he said.
  Apple refused Go Public's request for an interview and didn't respond to our specific questions about Noble's situation and its policies on digital assets.
      The company told Noble providing the password would   contravene a U.S. law —  the Electronic Communications Privacy Act — written back in 1986.
  The law, meant to protect Americans from unnecessary government and police surveillance, bars companies from giving out personal electronic information.
  But the decades-old law isn't meant for today's heavily online world of social media and other digital assets, Lown says.
      "[Yet] the service providers use it to their hearts' content … so they can say, sorry, I can't disclose this to you because that would be a breach of my obligations under the act."
  Citizens of countries that have laws specifically addressing digital assets — including those in the European Union — don't have the same problems. For those people, the  terms and conditions of Apple services grant jurisdiction to "the laws and courts of your usual place of residence."
    Some Canadians have done that. After Maureen Henry's adult son's body was found at a marina in Toronto in 2014 — a suspected suicide — she wanted access to his digital accounts to see if they would give her any answers.
  She got court orders, first from Ontario and later from a California court. Apple and Bell complied with the Ontario order, Google Canada fought it but gave her the data after Henry got the U.S. order. She's still fighting Facebook in court.
  More often though, Canadians are subject to U.S. laws, Lown says, even though proposed legislation that would protect people like Henry and Noble is already written and ready to be adopted.
    A proposed change to provincial inheritance laws, the  Uniform Access to Digital Assets by Fiduciaries Act, was put forward in 2016 by an  independent group of lawyers, judges and academics, of which Lown is a member.
  It would solve these sorts of problems for Canadians but, so far, only Saskatchewan has made it law.
    "This will force these giant tech companies to deal with the problem, because as it stands right now, good luck trying to get a hold of anyone in one of these companies who are almost always based in California."
  Only when Canada has that kind of "leverage," said Lown, will tech companies accommodate the needs of Canadians.
  Noble says Apple has a responsibility to be transparent and upfront about its policies when users sign up for the service, instead of putting that information in the fine print.
  She also wants to see  provincial and territorial governments do a better job of addressing the issue.
  "This is an area that needs to be addressed immediately … it's very important that people are able to access their loved ones' final photos and all kinds of things," she said.
  After Go Public contacted Apple, it reached out to Noble with details on what the court order needs to include.
  On a fixed income, she didn't think she could afford to do it and was ready to give up, until Sindhwani, her lawyer, offered to complete the application pro bono.
          We tell your stories, shed light on wrongdoing, and hold the powers that be accountable.
  If you have a story in the public interest, or if you're an insider with information, contact  gopublic@cbc.ca with your name, contact information and a brief summary. All emails are confidential until you decide to Go Public.

对我来说，禁闭没多久，星期五的啤酒会议就开始了。一位同事将它们安排为一种释放压力和社交的形式，因为我们都在为我们已经知道至少一年不会见到对方做准备-对于几周前刚刚开始在公司工作的人来说，我需要它。
在家工作一直是与世隔绝的，但在2020年变得更加孤立。对于我们这些过去全职在家工作的人来说--至少对于我们这些曾经这样做过、有吵吵闹闹的家庭和没有个人空间概念的孩子的人来说--在家庭生活和工作生活之间保持分离也变得困难得多。我们的配偶和孩子(在某些情况下，成年的孩子)都在同一时间在家，工作、学习或玩耍，或者只是在与我们相同的空间里呼吸太大声。
对于那些从来没有享受过其他人都不在家的时候在家办公的孤独的人来说，相信我：我们现在拥有的不是过去25年来我在家工作的样子。为了适应这一点，组织必须弄清楚如何在没有定期社交联系的情况下保持团队的凝聚力。他们还必须在善于沟通和干预员工家庭生活之间找到平衡，同时保持某种连贯的工作环境，这样人们就可以相互交谈，完成工作。
几乎可以肯定的是，未来的道路将是有节制的协作交流、有节制的社会化与工作日的整合以及猫模因的结合。
嗯，也许不太像猫模因那样-但在分布式工作场所建立一种正常运作的文化将需要旧办公室可能认为不利于生产力的东西。使用协作工具进行非正式交流和社交(加上猫表情包！)。是在办公室散居期间创造社区感的重要工具。
许多组织已经求助于协作工具来帮助弥合隔离并保持其员工的理智。这包括像啤酒电话会议这样的事情，但也包括其他破冰和互动，这些东西不属于这些技术曾经被定义为商业用途的范围。
协作工具可以为同事提供一种联系和重新创建社区感的方式，但也可以为组织提供一种方式来评估个人在家中或远程工作时面临的挑战，并帮助为他们提供一个平台，让他们分享关于如何应对和克服这些挑战的想法。好的工具还可以帮助建立工作流程的新常态，提供一种让员工建立新的流程和支持架构的方法，而这些流程和支持结构本来可能缺乏在家工作，并且可以找到新的工作方式，而不要求员工在标准的每天8小时工作时间内上班。
note_en:
The Friday "beer-thirty" Zoom conferences began for me not too long into the lockdown. A co-worker scheduled them as a form of stress release and socialization as we all prepared for what we already knew was going to be at least a year of not seeing each other in person—and for someone who had just started with the company a few weeks prior, I needed it.
 Working from home has always been isolating, but it has become even more so in 2020. And for those of us who've worked from home full-time in the past—well, at least for those of us who have done that and have loud families and kids with no concept of personal space—it has also become a lot harder to maintain a division between home life and work life. Our spouses and kids (and in some cases, adult kids) are all home at the same time, working or studying or playing or just  breathing too loudly in the same space as us.
  For those of you who've never enjoyed the solitude of a home office when everyone else is out of the house, trust me: what we have right now is  not what working at home has been like for the past 25 years for me. To adjust to this, organizations must figure out how to keep teams cohesive in the absence of regular social contact. They also must find a balance between being  communicative and being  intrusive into the home life of employees, all while still keeping some kind of coherent work environment going so people can talk to each other and get work done.
 The way forward is almost certainly going to be a combination of measured collaborative communications, measured integration of socialization into the work day, and cat memes.
  Well, maybe not so much with the cat memes—but establishing a functioning culture in a distributed workplace will require things that the old office likely would consider detrimental to productivity. Informal communications and socialization using collaboration tools (plus cat memes!) are important tools to create a sense of community during the office diaspora.
  Many organizations have turned to collaborative tools to help bridge the isolation and maintain the sanity of their employees. This includes things like the "beer-thirty" conference call but also other ice-breakers and interactions that do not fall within what were once defined as "business use" of those technologies.
 Collaboration tools can give co-workers a way to bond and re-create a sense of community, but they can also provide a way for organizations to gauge the challenges that individuals are having working from home or remotely—and help give them a platform for sharing ideas about how to deal with and overcome those challenges. Good tools can also help establish a new normal for workflow, providing a way to let employees build new processes and support structures that working from home may otherwise lack—and to find new ways to work that don't require employees to be "on" for a standard eight-hour work day.

note:
据英国《每日电讯报》1日报道，欧盟正在牵头两项积极调查，调查Instagram是否非法泄露了数百万未成年用户的个人信息。
调查由爱尔兰数据保护委员会(DPC)进行，该委员会是Instagram在欧洲的母公司Facebook的官方数据监管机构。Facebook的欧洲总部设在都柏林。
DPC在收到美国数据科学家大卫·斯蒂尔(David Stier)的投诉后，于上月启动了调查。斯特尔告诉“每日电讯报”，他相信多达500万18岁以下的用户的个人联系方式被曝光。
斯特尔说，Instagram拥有巨大的资源可供他们使用，但这起事件表明，他们对用户的同理心、安全意识和关爱程度低得可怜。
DPC副专员格雷厄姆·多伊尔在接受“每日电讯报”采访时表示，该委员会一直在积极监控收到的来自该领域个人的投诉，并发现了Instagram上儿童个人数据处理方面的潜在问题，需要进一步审查。
DPC的第一项调查将集中在Instagram是否促进了用户隐私，特别是儿童用户的隐私。第二个将特别关注其商业账户功能是否适合显示未成年用户的联系方式。
据“每日电讯报”报道，根据欧盟的数据保护条例，每项调查最高可导致Facebook年收入4%的罚款。Facebook&2019年的年营业额为707亿美元，这意味着最高4%的罚款将相当于28亿美元。
斯特尔在2019年的一篇中型帖子中写道，他发现了Instagram的一个漏洞，该漏洞允许未成年用户的个人数据公开曝光。
该漏洞与Instagram如何允许用户在普通账户和商务账户之间切换有关。要转换为拥有商业账户，Instagram用户必须添加电话号码或电子邮件地址，然后这些地址就可以公开访问。
斯蒂尔在2019年写道，因为对谁可以将个人资料更改为商业账户似乎没有限制，许多孩子已经意识到，他们可以申请拥有一家企业，这样他们就可以在自己的个人资料页面上添加联系人按钮。
自那以后，Instagram改变了这一流程，因此商业账户持有人必须选择公开展示他们的联系方式。
我们一直很清楚，当人们选择在Instagram上开设商业账户时，他们分享的联系信息将被公开展示。Instagram的一位发言人告诉“商业内幕”，这和暴露人们的信息有很大不同。
他们补充说：我们正在与IDPC保持密切联系，我们正在配合他们的调查。

note_en:
The EU is leading two active investigations into whether Instagram illegally exposed the personal details of millions of underage users, the  Telegraph reported Monday.
 The investigations are being carried out by Ireland's Data Protection Commission (DPC), the official data regulator for Instagram's parent company Facebook in Europe. Facebook's European headquarters are in Dublin.
 The DPC launched the investigations last month after it received a complaint from US data scientist David Stier. Stier told the Telegraph he believes as many as 5 million users under the age of 18 had their personal contact details exposed.
 "Instagram had enormous resources at their disposal, but this incident shows they had woefully low levels of empathy, safety awareness and care for their users," Stier said.
   DPC Deputy Commissioner Graham Doyle told the Telegraph that the commission "has been actively monitoring complaints received from individuals in this area and has identified potential concerns in relation to the processing of children's personal data on Instagram which require further examination."
 The first of the DPC's investigations will focus on whether Instagram promotes user privacy, especially for child users. The second will focus specifically on whether it's appropriate for its business accounts feature to display the contact details of underage users.
 The Telegraph reports that under EU data protection regulations, each investigation could result in a maximum fine of 4% of Facebook's annual revenue. Facebook's annual turnover for 2019 was $70.7 billion, meaning a maximum 4% fine would equal $2.8 billion.
  Stier wrote in a  Medium post in 2019 that he had found an Instagram loophole that allowed the personal data of underage users to be publicly exposed.
   The loophole is linked to how Instagram allows users to switch between a regular account and a "business" account. To change over to having a business account, Instagram users must add either a phone number or an email address, which was then publicly accessible.
 "Because there are seemingly no restrictions on who can change their personal profile to a business account, many kids have figured out that they can 'claim' to have a business so that they can add the contact buttons onto their own profile page," Stier wrote in 2019.
 Instagram has since changed this process so business account holders have to opt-in to having their contact details publicly displayed.
 "We've always been clear that when people choose to set up a business account on Instagram, the contact information they shared would be publicly displayed. That's very different to exposing people's information," an Instagram spokesperson told Business Insider.
   "We're in close contact with the IDPC and we're cooperating with their inquiries," they added.

title: 欧盟正在调查Instagram涉嫌泄露多达500万未成年用户的联系方式
title_en: The EU is investigating Instagram for allegedly exposing the contact data of up to 5 million underage users

这轮融资包括Iteration、Hustle Fund、XA Network、Rapzo Capital、Lazada联合创始人斯坦·雅卡博(Stein Jakabo)和Dot Property创始人本·内夫(Ben Neve)的投资。普罗普赛尔还表示，“三个未披露的高度战略性投资者”和回归的私人投资者参与其中。
Propsell上一次宣布融资是在2018年12月，当时它筹集了100万新元(约合73.7万美元)的种子资金。
Propsell成立于2018年，并于次年推出。该公司表示，其技术平台能够更快地完成交易，帮助完成房地产估值等任务，并将标准佣金从2%降至1%，因为初创公司的内部代理能够在更短的时间内完成更多交易。
该公司声称，目前每年处理价值约7500万新元的物业。在大流行期间，在线仪表盘和虚拟查看等科技驱动的服务使Propsell的代理人能够继续与客户合作。
尽管新冠肺炎对经济造成影响，但新加坡房地产市场预计将相对较快复苏，尤其是住宅领域，因为对新公寓的需求和外国投资。
另一家新加坡专注于房地产的初创公司最近筹集了资金，那就是PropertyGuru。上个月，房地产上市平台宣布KKR和TPG投资2.2亿美元，以拓展新的东南亚亚洲市场。PropertyGuru最直接的竞争对手是99.co，但Propsell、Ohmyhome和Greyloft等提供与技术平台相结合的代理服务的初创公司，都在新加坡房地产市场提供了另一种选择。
迭代合伙人(也是旧金山Proptech初创公司Divvy Homes的创始人)布赖恩·马在一份关于其对Propsell的投资的新闻声明中表示：“在全球范围内，现代房地产经纪公司已经在以惊人的速度夺取市场份额。在新加坡这样一个楼价高企、需要高质素服务的市场，我们相信数码化是无可避免的。我们为Propsell在那里带头冲锋而感到兴奋。“
note_en:
The round included investment from Iterative; Hustle Fund; XA Network; Rapzo Capital; Lazada co-founder Stein Jakabo; and Dot  Property   founder Ben Neve.  Propseller   also said “three undisclosed highly strategic investors” and returning private investors participated.
 Propseller’s last funding announcement was in December 2018, when it  raised SGD $1 million (about $737,000) in seed funding.
  Founded in 2018 and launched the next year, Propseller says its technology platform enables transactions to close more quickly, helping with tasks like property valuations, and reduces standard commission fees to 1% from 2% because the startup’s in-house agents are able to finish more transactions in less time.
 The company claims it is currently handling about SGD $75 million worth of properties each year. During the pandemic, tech-enabled services like online dashboards and virtual viewings have allowed Propseller’s agents to continue working with clients.
 Despite the economic impact of COVID-19, Singapore’s  real estate market is expected to recover relatively quickly, especially the residential sector, because of demand for new condominiums and foreign investment.
  Another Singaporean real estate-focused startup that recently raised funding is PropertyGuru. Last month, the property listing platform announced an investment of $220 million from KKR and TPG to expand into new Southast Asian markets. PropertyGuru’s most direct competitor is 99.co, but startups like Propseller, Ohmyhome and Greyloft, which offer agent services combined with tech platforms, all provide an alternative in the Singaporean real estate market.
 In a press statement about its investment in Propseller, Iterative partner (and founder of Divvy Homes, a San Francisco-based proptech startup) Brian Ma said, “Worldwide, modern estate agencies are already taking market share at breakneck speeds. In a market like Singapore with high property prices and the need for high quality service, we believe digitalization will be inevitable. We’re excited for Propseller to lead the charge there.”
title: 新加坡科技房地产经纪公司Propsell筹集120万美元种子轮
